brand.name: Venturi Labs
brand.essence: Venturi Labs sits at the intersection of academic rigor and industrial execution. The visual identity must communicate Precision, Modularity, and Compounding Efficiency.
brand.vibe: Modern, high-end European deep tech studio. Not a flashy consumer SaaS, nor a dusty legacy academic portal.
brand.design_philosophy: Rooted in Dutch design principles—pragmatic, minimalist, grid-oriented, and ruthlessly functional. Every visual element must serve a purpose.

primary_logo.symbolism: Symbolizes the coming together of disparate academic parts into a stable, commercial whole. Interlocking blocks represent the compounding architecture; the cyan junction represents the point of valorisation.

iconography_style.guidelines: Avoid filled or 'bubbly' icons. Should look drafted in CAD or drawn on an optical table layout. Use sharp 90-degree angles and precise curves.

foundation_hardware.name: Slate 900
foundation_hardware.usage: Primary text and dark-mode backgrounds. Represents deep, anodized aluminum black.

foundation_hardware.name: Slate 600
foundation_hardware.usage: Secondary text and subtle borders. Represents gunmetal gray.

foundation_hardware.name: Slate 50
foundation_hardware.usage: Primary light background. Represents clean room white.

primary_accent.name: Venturi Cyan
primary_accent.usage: Primary call-to-action buttons, active states, and highlighting key data points. Represents precision optics, clean UI/UX, and scientific accuracy.

structural_accents.name: Architecture Indigo
structural_accents.usage: Categorizing website sections related to the software core, Python data flows, and telemetry.

structural_accents.name: Ecosystem Emerald
structural_accents.usage: Categorizing website sections related to regional development, job creation, and sustainable funding.

primary.description: Highly legible, geometric sans-serif fonts that look exceptional on digital screens and technical dashboards. Communicates transparency and modern efficiency.

secondary.description: Monospaced fonts for displaying numbers, technical specifications (DAQ frequencies, BOM costs), and UI elements mimicking the internal PyQt interface. Signals deep software and data analysis capability.

imagery_direction.avoid: Generic business stock photos (e.g., people in suits shaking hands, generic test tubes).

themes.name: Macro-Engineering
themes.description: Extreme close-ups of complex geometries, high-resolution shots of custom PCB traces, raw machined aluminum housings, and intense localized light of spark ablation processes.

themes.name: The 'Science Breadboard'
themes.description: Precise alignment of optical components on a breadboard or the clean layout of a microfluidic chip.

themes.name: UI/UX as Art
themes.description: Abstract, high-contrast snippets of the Venturi Software Core. Clean data visualization graphs and sleek PyQt dashboard interfaces used as background textures.

imagery_direction.treatment: Slightly desaturated, cool-toned grade to match the Slate/Cyan palette, ensuring hardware looks premium and industrial.

ui_ux_principles.description: The website should feel slightly like an instrument control panel. Use subtle containment borders, distinct modular cards for different concepts, and crisp hover states.
Offer a sleek, Slate-900 dark mode to cater to academics and engineers who work in dark labs or use dark-themed IDEs.
Animations should be snappy and linear (e.g., 150ms ease-out), mimicking the zero-latency feel of a well-optimized DAQ system. Avoid slow, bouncy, 'creative' animations.

traits.description: Let the efficiency of the platform do the talking.
Use exact terminology. E.g., 'we build the PyQt UI, DAQ drivers, and physical enclosures' instead of 'we build tech'.
Address PIs and engineers as equals. Read like a whitepaper written by an engineer who understands both laser alignment and university TTO frameworks.
